summary: Made trends interest/related/region/changes/opportunities/seasonality return notFoundErr (exit 3) instead of exit 0 when a live/local query genuinely has no data, distinct from a query that succeeds with real results.
reason: Live dogfood's error-path check expects a non-zero exit for a garbage keyword argument on non-search-verb commands. Google Trends keyword search has no concept of an 'invalid keyword' -- any string is a valid search term -- so the fix is not to reject input, but to distinguish 'queried successfully, zero data points' from 'queried successfully, got real data' via exit code (the same convention grep/ripgrep use for no-matches). trends region needed a different signal than empty output: Google's comparedgeo response always covers every geo in scope even for a nonsense keyword, with relative-scaled noise producing misleading non-zero 'peak' values (e.g. Paraguay:100 for a garbage keyword) -- so region additionally fetches the TIMESERIES widget from the same explore() call and uses its emptiness as the reliable 'no real data' signal instead of the region values themselves.
validated_outcome: Live dogfood full matrix: 65/65 tests pass (up from 59/65). Manually confirmed both directions: trends interest/region coffee still exit 0 with real data; trends interest/region __printing_press_invalid__ now exit 3 with a clear message.
